what_it_does: Stores an array of extracted design findings about typography, type hierarchy, and text-budget rules observed across several pitch-deck sources, pairing each observation with a recipe, evidence, generator rule, source, and compression depth.
observations: Each finding is a JSON object with category, name, recipe, evidence, emergent flag, generator_rule, _src, and _depth.
Categories are mostly type-hierarchy, plus emergent-content-budget and one emergent-narrative-title-chain entry.
Sources referenced include capital-raise, company-info, deck1-2026, and pitch-deck at different page depths.
Many roles are documented repeatedly for different sources with varying specs (title alignment, stat color, button style).
Gold is consistently used as an accent for section labels, stats, and brand marks, but exact shade and usage differ by source.
Strict one-line and character-budget rules are treated as generator constraints, not just observations.
The final object is truncated mid-recipe.
standouts: Titles form a single continuous grammatical sentence across the deck (emergent-narrative-title-chain).
Strict one-line rule for all headings, labels, buttons, and bullets.
Intentional text-length calibration so list items stay within ~20-30% of median length.
Oversized gold statistic numbers act as visual anchors.
Golden arrow/triangle bullet marker used as a brand glyph.
Status dot is the only permitted use of green.
ConceptV logotype split: dark 'Concept' plus golden 'V' mark.
conventions: Findings follow a fixed schema: category, name, recipe, evidence, emergent, generator_rule, _src, _depth.
Recipe describes visual attributes in approximate values; generator_rule states actionable constraints.
Evidence cites slide page identifiers (p-02, p18, etc.).
emergent=true marks patterns that arise from authoring discipline rather than explicit style rules.
Tone is neutral and descriptive, avoiding evaluative language.
_src and _depth provide provenance and compression context.
concerns: The file ends with an incomplete/truncated finding.
Multiple source-specific entries for the same role (e.g., slide title, section subheading) contain conflicting specs, with no reconciliation field.
Color and size values are approximate, which may limit precision for automated generation.
No schema version or generation timestamp is present.
Some entries mix typography with broader content/narrative patterns under the 'type' umbrella.
notes: A source-aware typographic rule ledger that tries to turn observed pitch-deck text styling into explicit, generatable constraints.
questions: Which source should be treated as authoritative when specs conflict?
What tool or process produced these findings and how were emergent flags decided?
How are approximate color/size values verified against the original decks?
What is the intended consumer: a generator, a linter, or a design-system reference?
What does the '_faces' directory signify in the broader pipeline?
How should the truncated final entry be completed or removed?
purpose_vs_actual: The file documents extracted type and text-budget findings with generator rules, which it does; it also folds in higher-level narrative and content-budget patterns under the 'type' heading, slightly broadening the stated scope.
apparent_themes: Content length as a first-class design variable
Gold as a semantic accent for labels, data, and brand identity
One-line discipline for headings and labels
Source-specific design languages at different compression depths
Emergent patterns versus explicit style rules
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to reverse-engineer a reusable, machine-readable design language from pitch-deck visuals so that slide text, hierarchy, color, and narrative flow can be generated or audited automatically.
novelty: The emergent-narrative-title-chain finding is unusual—treating slide titles as one continuous grammatical sentence—and the strict, source-specific character-budget generator rules derived from visual observation are more granular than typical style guides.
contribution: Provides granular, source-aware typographic constraints and emergent authoring disciplines (e.g., title chains, one-line labels, calibrated bullet lengths) that a fused system could keep as reference material for generating or auditing slide text.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON ledger of extracted typographic and text-budget findings from multiple pitch-deck sources, pairing observed visual recipes with generator rules and source/depth metadata.
intention_for_embedding: To codify source-specific type hierarchy, color, sizing, alignment, and content-budget rules into machine-readable design constraints for slide generation or design review.
